content: State-of-the-art 2024–2025 active learning agent memory systems can be restructured to prioritize the retention of *unresolved prediction errors* generated by curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods like ICM and RND, rather than just storing frequent or recent experiences. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where an agent’s curiosity continuously refines the most information-dense parts of its memory, which in turn boosts its active learning sample efficiency by focusing queries exclusively on under-explored memory gaps.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
